patch.value: SDK extension hook context and manifest capability guardrails

Implement GitHub #103 and #104 as one SDK/package-platform slice: enrich onWrite hook context with item identity/content for sync integrations, and make missing manifest capabilities actionable before packages ship broken. Duplicate check: searched onWrite hook identity/content and manifest capability omission; only closed historical lifecycle/capability items found, no open canonical item.

author: codex-active-sdk-cli
message: Start active SDK/package-platform implementation. Scope combines GitHub #103 missing manifest capability guardrails, GitHub #104 richer onWrite context, failed Auto Release triage, dependency/security checks, temp-project dogfood, changelog, PR review loop, and merge-back.

value.author: codex-active-sdk-cli
value.text: Investigation: gh issue #104 needs OnWriteHookContext enrichment. Existing runtime only passes path/scope/op from create, mutateItem, deleteItem, restore, and non-item writes. Plan: add optional item_id/item_type/before/after/changed_fields to SDK type and item mutation hook calls while preserving existing hooks. GitHub issue #103 maps to existing assertExtensionCapability TypeError during activation; improvement is to attach structured trace metadata and doctor remediation naming missing capability. Auto Release run 26870058549 failed because SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN was empty before release pipeline execution; separate configuration gate, not a code regression.

value.author: subagent-onwrite
value.text:
Sub-agent findings for GitHub #104 onWrite context enrichment (read-only exploration, no file edits):

Current type/export path:
- src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts:225-229 defines OnWriteHookContext as { path, scope, op } only; OnWriteHook is typed at 241-244.
- src/core/extensions/loader.ts re-exports the type from extension-types and registers api.hooks.onWrite with hooks capability/policy checks at 1938-1948; no loader change is needed except any docs/tests around the API contract.
- src/core/extensions/extension-hook-runtime.ts:37-49 clones hook context before dispatch; 67-69 invokes onWrite. Additive fields must remain structured-clone-safe.
- src/core/extensions/index.ts:102-107 is the active runtime wrapper.
- src/sdk/index.ts:89-167 re-exports OnWriteHookContext and ItemDocument/ItemFrontMatter, so SDK consumers get the enriched shape automatically after type change.

Recommended type shape:
- Add optional fields to OnWriteHookContext to preserve existing hooks: item_id?: string; item_type?: ItemType; item?: ItemFrontMatter; body?: string; document?: ItemDocument; before?: ItemDocument; after?: ItemDocument.
- Keep path/scope/op required and unchanged. For history-file events, include item identity and before/after/document when the caller already has it, but avoid requiring item payload for non-item writes.
- If avoiding duplicate payload is preferred, choose a lean contract: item_id?: string; item_type?: ItemType; item?: ItemFrontMatter; body?: string; before?: ItemDocument; after?: ItemDocument. document can be omitted because after is the post-write document for create/update/close/restore/import and before is the deleted document for delete.

Runtime hook invocation sites to update for item identity/content:
- src/cli/commands/create.ts:1904-1915: create item file + create:history. Caller has id, type, beforeDocument, afterDocument. Add item_id/id, item_type/type, item/body/document/after on both item and history hook contexts; before is emptyImported-style document.
- src/core/store/item-store.ts:484-495: mutateItem item write + history write. This covers update, close, claim/release, comments/notes/learnings, append, files/docs/tests, plan mutations, etc. Caller has located.id, beforeDocument, afterDocument, effectiveTargetItemPath, historyPath. Add identity and before/after/post item content to both hook contexts.
- src/core/store/item-store.ts:601-612: delete item removal + delete:history. Caller has located.id, beforeDocument, tombstoneDocument, effectiveItemPath, historyPath. Add identity and before document; after should be tombstone/empty document. item/body/document can represent beforeDocument or be omitted for delete; safest explicit contract is before/after.
- src/cli/commands/restore.ts:453-464: restore item write + restore:history. Caller has resolvedId, restoredDocument, resolvedCurrentDocument. Enrich both contexts.
- src/sdk/package-import-adapters.ts:182-193: import item write + import:history. Caller has id and document; enrich as import create-like event if #104 wants package sync integrations to see imported items too.

Do not blindly enrich non-item write hooks unless optional metadata is available:
- src/core/lock/lock.ts:99-104 lock create/remove; no item document available.
- src/core/history/history-stream-policy.ts:40-48 auto-create empty history stream; has itemId but no document.
- src/core/store/settings.ts:680-687, src/cli/commands/schema.ts:165-170, src/core/store/item-format-migration.ts:106-126, src/cli/commands/gc.ts:105-109, src/cli/commands/history-repair.ts:211-216, src/cli/commands/history-redact.ts:599-614, init/reindex hook sites: either not item CRUD or need separate careful treatment.

Item-store mutation paths affected by mutateItem:
- src/cli/commands/update.ts:1507-1518 op update/update_audit.
- src/cli/commands/close.ts:125-133 op close.
- src/cli/commands/delete.ts:36-44 routes to deleteItem.
- src/cli/commands/claim.ts ops claim/release; annotation-command.ts comments/notes/learnings via configured op; append.ts op append; linked-artifacts.ts/files.ts docs/files ops; test.ts tests_add; src/core/test/item-test-run-tracking.ts op test_run_track; plan.ts plan_* ops.

Tests to update/add:
- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts:4838-4958 currently logs write op/path only. Extend the temp extension to log event.item_id/event.item?.title/event.body or before/after metadata, then assert create, update, and delete/close coverage. Add delete and close in this test or a focused new integration test.
- tests/unit/extensions-runtime.spec.ts:53-109 should assert optional enriched fields pass through active wrapper and hook runtime clone behavior remains isolated; also keep existing minimal {path,scope,op} calls compiling.
- tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts:1448-1452 can keep minimal shape; optionally add an enriched runOnWriteHooks invocation to prove direct registry dispatch accepts it.
- tests/unit/item-store.spec.ts should add focused mutateItem/deleteItem hook payload tests for before/after/document and rollback non-interference.
- tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts around 89-167/610-640 should compile/import the enriched OnWriteHookContext shape from SDK.

Docs/examples to update:
- docs/EXTENSIONS.md:274 should describe onWrite context fields and note item fields are optional because onWrite also observes settings, locks, schema, history, init, gc, and reindex writes.
- docs/SDK.md:300-317 should document the typed context and backwards-compatible optionality.
- docs/examples/starter-extension/index.js:125-127 and README can show optional item_id usage without requiring it.
- packages/pm-lifecycle-hooks README/index can remain default-inert unless the exemplar should demonstrate optional item-aware observation.

Compatibility risks:
- Existing hooks expecting only path/scope/op are safe if fields are additive and optional.
- Do not make item_id/item/document required: many current onWrite call sites are non-item writes.
- Context cloning uses structuredClone-like helper; ItemDocument is plain data, but keep payload plain and avoid functions/classes.
- Payload size may grow on every mutation, especially history events and large bodies. Consider before/after only for item CRUD/mutate paths, and avoid adding documents to broad non-item hooks.
- Delete semantics need clear docs: post-write item file is gone, so consumers should use before for deleted content and after as an empty/tombstone document.

value.author: subagent-capability
value.text:
Subagent capability map for GitHub #103 (read-only; no source edits):

Runtime capability gate map:
- Canonical capabilities live in src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts:9-21: commands, renderers, hooks, schema, importers, search, parser, preflight, services; policy surfaces are src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts:39-59. Unknown/legacy manifest capability normalization and warning formatting is src/core/extensions/extension-capability-aliases.ts:17-58 and :115-128.
- Manifest parsing normalizes capabilities but does not reject unknown names at scan time: src/core/extensions/loader.ts:379-388. Discovery emits extension_capability_unknown / extension_capability_legacy_alias warnings from src/core/extensions/loader.ts:611-619.
- Actual omitted-capability enforcement is activation-time in src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1361-1388 via assertExtensionCapability(). It throws TypeError when a register* API is used without the matching manifest capability. Direct unit fixtures with no capabilities array are intentionally grandfathered by declared === null.
- register* to capability mapping in src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1454-1964: registerCommand -> commands; registerCommand definition.flags -> schema; registerParser -> parser; registerPreflight -> preflight; registerService -> services; registerRenderer -> renderers; registerFlags/registerItemFields/registerItemTypes/registerMigration -> schema; registerImporter/registerExporter -> importers; importer/exporter options.flags -> schema; registerSearchProvider/registerVectorStoreAdapter -> search; api.hooks.beforeCommand/afterCommand/onWrite/onRead/onIndex -> hooks.
- Policy gating is separate and happens after capability assertion: createExtensionApi allowRegistration uses evaluateExtensionPolicyForRegistration at src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1425-1438, with policy normalization/evaluation in src/core/extensions/extension-policy.ts. Policy can warn/block allowed/blocked capabilities/surfaces/commands/actions/services, but it does not replace manifest capability enforcement.

Activation failure surfacing:
- activateExtensions catches thrown registration errors and adds extension_activate_failed:<layer>:<name>, plus failed[] entries with layer/name/entry_path/error and optional trace at src/core/extensions/loader.ts:2145-2188. Collision warnings are merged afterward at src/core/extensions/loader.ts:2191-2224.
- Doctor runtime state marks failed activations runtime_active=false, activation_status=failed in src/cli/commands/extension/doctor.ts:21-115. Doctor summary then exposes activation_failure_count, blocking_failure_count, has_blocking_failures, warning_codes, remediation, capability_contract, and capability_guidance in src/cli/commands/extension.ts:1439-1551. Deep/trace output includes activation.failed and trace.activation_failures in src/cli/commands/extension.ts:1579-1640.
- Current gap: malformed registerCommand has rich trace via createRegistrationValidationError, but missing-capability TypeErrors from assertExtensionCapability do not attach a structured trace, so doctor can show the error string but cannot show method/capability/remediation in trace.activation_failures.

Package doctor / release gate locations:
- package doctor is the package vocabulary over extension doctor; strict/fail-on-warn are already accepted only for doctor in src/cli/commands/extension.ts:899-917 and tested in tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts:1119-1266.
- Doctor triage/remediation logic is src/cli/commands/extension/doctor.ts:252-420; unknown capability guidance only covers unknown manifest capability names today, not omitted required capabilities.
- Package-first dogfood currently asserts package --doctor exists and runs package doctor summary, but without strict exit: scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs:290-355.
- Release gates live in scripts/release/run-gates.mjs:45-151 and include build/typecheck/docs/static/coverage/version/security/npx/package-first dogfood/npm-pack/compatibility/sentry. release:pipeline delegates to run-gates from scripts/release/run-release-pipeline.mjs:158-166. CI release workflow also runs package-first dogfood before publish in .github/workflows/release.yml:143-150. Auto release workflow builds before running run-release-pipeline in .github/workflows/auto-release.yml:90-131. package.json scripts are at package.json:73-95.

Smallest robust implementation for omitted capabilities failing before publish:
1) In src/core/extensions/loader.ts, replace bare missing-capability TypeError with a registration validation error carrying trace { method, registration_index: current relevant registry length or 0, capability, expected_schema: 'manifest capabilities must include <capability>', hint: 'Add <capability> to manifest.json capabilities or remove the <method> registration.' }. Also pass through capability in doctor trace serialization at src/cli/commands/extension.ts:1625-1639. This makes failures actionable without changing runtime semantics.
2) Add doctor remediation for missing required manifest capabilities. Either parse activation failed errors that match /requires capability '([^']+)'/ into a new warning code such as extension_capability_missing:<layer>:<name>:<capability>:method=<method>, or derive it from the new trace. Then buildExtensionTriageSummary can say exactly which capability to add and recommend pm package doctor --project --detail deep --trace.
3) Make package-first dogfood fail on activation failures by changing scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs:355 to run package doctor --project --detail deep --trace --strict-exit. This is low blast-radius because it already installs all first-party package aliases into sandboxed PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH and is already in release:gates unless --skip-dogfood is passed.
4) Add a cheap static first-party guard so skipped dogfood does not hide manifest drift: in tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts or a new unit spec, scan each packages/pm-*/extensions/*/index.ts and index.js for API usage tokens and assert manifest.json capabilities include required capabilities. Mapping: registerCommand->commands; registerFlags/registerItemFields/registerItemTypes/registerMigration and options.flags->schema; registerImporter/registerExporter->importers; registerSearchProvider/registerVectorStoreAdapter->search; registerParser->parser; registerPreflight->preflight; registerService->services; registerRenderer->renderers; api.hooks.*->hooks. Existing tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts:499-513 only checks index.js exported capabilities match manifest.json, not API usage vs manifest.

Tests to add/update:
- Update tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts around the existing omitted-capability test at :2978-3109 to assert each failed entry has trace.method and trace.capability.
- Add/update tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts near doctor trace coverage (:1822-1877) with a fixture that declares commands but calls registerFlags or omits commands and calls registerCommand; assert pm package/extension doctor --detail deep --trace reports method, capability, and actionable remediation, and --strict-exit exits nonzero.
- Update scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs expectation if switching doctor to --strict-exit; optionally add an integration regression in tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts near :1119-1266 for package doctor strict missing capability.
- Add static manifest/API usage test in tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts after the shipped module capability alignment test (:499-513).
- Update release automation contract tests if run-gates or dogfood command strings change: tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts and tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts currently assert release script strings around release:gates and packaging guards.

Risks / edge cases:
- Static source scanning can false-positive on comments/examples or miss computed API access; keep it first-party-only and conservative, with runtime doctor strict as the real enforcement.
- registerCommand definition.flags and importer/exporter options.flags require schema in addition to commands/importers; tests need explicit coverage because this is the easiest omission to miss.
- Some tests construct LoadedExtension fixtures without capabilities; keep the declared === null compatibility path unless refactoring those fixtures.
- Doctor strict currently treats all warnings as failures; making dogfood doctor strict may expose unrelated package warnings. Prefer sandboxed package-first dogfood and keep normal local pm package doctor non-strict by default.

Auto Release run 26870058549 classification:
- Separate from GitHub #103 capability enforcement. gh run view shows Auto Release on schedule, head main 61a2034dc43137a1e5e99dda972e7b5d28412a25, failed in job 79242893182 step 'Run auto release pipeline'. Logs show build succeeded, then the workflow exited before run-release-pipeline because INPUT_PUSH=true, INPUT_DRY_RUN=false, and SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN was empty. Exact log lines: 'SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN is required before Auto Release can create a version commit/tag.' and 'The downstream Release workflow treats the Sentry reliability gate as mandatory; failing here prevents unpublished tags.' The guard is .github/workflows/auto-release.yml:114-118. No package doctor, dogfood, release gates, or publish step ran in that failed run.

Local verification run by subagent: npm install -g .; pm --version => 2026.6.2; node -v => v25.9.0; pnpm -v => 10.33.4; pnpm build passed; PM_AUTHOR=subagent-capability pm package doctor --project --detail deep --trace --json returned status ok with activation_failure_count=0 and blocking_failure_count=0.

value.author: codex-active-sdk-cli
value.text: Evidence: Implemented optional item-aware onWrite context for create/update/delete/restore/import item writes; added structured missing-capability activation traces plus doctor warning/remediation; hardened package-first dogfood to assert no activation/blocking/missing-capability failures; added first-party manifest/API capability guard; updated commander to 15.0.0 after dependency triage. Verification passed: pnpm build; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts tests/unit/help-runtime.spec.ts tests/unit/config-positional-value.spec.ts tests/unit/item-store.spec.ts tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts (5 files, 112 tests); node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/item-store.spec.ts tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts (3 files, 85 tests); npm run dogfood:package-first (87 commands in temp project); pnpm audit --audit-level moderate && pnpm outdated --format json (no vulnerabilities, no outdated packages); pnpm changelog:pm:check (pm-changelog 2026.6.3, CHANGELOG unchanged before close). GitHub checks: no open PRs at start; issues #103/#104 are covered by this item; Auto Release run 26870058549 failure is separate missing SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN config.

value.author: codex-active-sdk-cli
value.text: Review follow-up: addressed CodeRabbit actionable comment by narrowing package-manifest source-read fallback to ENOENT only, so real read errors are rethrown. Gemini optional-chaining recommendation was reviewed and not applied because the affected documents are constructed/loaded invariants at those call sites. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts; pnpm build.

patch.value: pnpm audit --audit-level moderate && pnpm outdated --format json

value.author: codex-active-sdk-cli
value.text: Review follow-up 2: CodeRabbit re-review confirmed ENOENT fix and flagged commander 15 Node >=22.12.0 compatibility plus low-risk SDK clarity gaps. Reverted commander to 14.0.3 to preserve package.json Node >=20 and CI Node 20 support. Documented registration_index=-1 as pre-registration capability-gate trace sentinel, documented changed_fields lifecycle sentinels for import/restore/delete, and annotated package-manifest source scan as heuristic with package doctor as runtime guard. Gemini beforeDocument ReferenceError claim was checked against current create.ts and is not valid because beforeDocument is declared before use. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts; pnpm build.

value.author: codex-active-sdk-cli
value.text: Review follow-up 3: Gemini's latest finding targeted pm-changelog --section-by label duplicate tags, not pm-cli core. Verified in upstream /home/steve/GITHUB_RELEASE/pm-changelog, created tracked item pmc-an10, fixed label grouping to deduplicate normalized tags per item, added regression coverage, verified npm test (64 tests), and opened upstream PR https://github.com/unbraind/pm-changelog/pull/25. No vendored pm-cli package files were patched for this package-owned defect.
